demographics.living_situation: lives at home with spouse; spouse is present and made the call

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I woke up gasping, and I feel like I am drowning from the inside; I can't breathe unless I sit straight up! (Spouse on the line: he woke up about an hour and a half ago fighting for air and now he's coughing up this pink frothy stuff.)
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: Woke abruptly in the middle of the night unable to breathe, has to sit bolt upright, and is now coughing up pink frothy fluid — it is getting worse, not better.

symptom_narrative.onset: woke suddenly about 90 minutes ago gasping for air
symptom_narrative.duration: about an hour and a half and getting worse
symptom_narrative.character: feels like drowning from the inside; a continuous cough that over the last half hour has started bringing up frothy, pink, blood-tinged fluid; tight chest and a frightening sense that something terrible is about to happen
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the worst breathing he has ever had — cannot speak more than a few words, exhausted from the effort
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: chest — tightness across the chest and cannot get air in
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: came on abruptly out of sleep and has been constant and worsening since
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: the only thing that helps a little is sitting bolt upright on the edge of the bed
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: lying back down makes it dramatically worse
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: cold sweaty clammy skin, frothy pink sputum, chest tightness, feeling of impending doom
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: spouse not aware of a night this severe before
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: cannot walk or lie down; sitting upright at the edge of the bed, unable to do anything but concentrate on breathing

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: fluid problem — takes a water tablet to stop fluid building up
past_medical_history.how_managed: furosemide daily, but he missed yesterday's dose
has a CPAP machine but did not wear it tonight

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives with spouse who is present and assisting; has a CPAP machine at home for sleep apnoea that was not used tonight

objective_data_offered.value: sitting bolt upright on the edge of the bed, visibly fighting for breath and using neck and shoulder muscles to breathe, only able to get a few words out at a time
coughing up frothy, pink, blood-tinged fluid
skin looks pale, cold, sweaty and clammy
both lower legs look puffy and swollen up to about mid-calf
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: carer eyeball estimate, not a graded clinical assessment
self-reported usual weight, not measured this encounter
